nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: You move quietly for a house cat. Good. I've been mapping the territories and there's work that needs doing — the kind of work that doesn't get done through bravery alone. Eyes, ears, and careful paws.

options.text: Why are you doing this alone?

nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: Reconnaissance, mostly. Something is shifting the borders between the clans — pawprints where none should be, patrols arriving late, scents that don't match any clan I know. If you can walk a few paths for me and report what you see, I'll make it worth your time.

options.text: How do I know I can trust you?

nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: Clan scouts only see clan borders. A cat without a clan sees the whole map. I answer to no leader, which means I can tell you things the four leaders wouldn't admit to each other.

nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: Here — the four clan territories, the river, the burned woods, the frost ridge. The markings you don't recognize? Those are the problem. Fresh, faint, wrong-shaped. Something doesn't belong.

options.text: I'll help you find out what.

nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: Start by checking the markers near the river crossing. Don't engage anyone. Just look, remember, return. Quest accepted — blow the bone whistle if you need me.

nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: You don't, and you shouldn't — not fully. But I don't lie about the job. If what I ask you to look at isn't there, I pay you anyway for the walk. Fair?

nodes.speaker: Pathfinder
nodes.text: The map will be here when you're ready. Keep your ears up.
